sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK, so the left eye is now at (9, 44) and the right eye is at (39, 44). Both eyes are at col 44, but at different rows (9 and 39).
Looking at the current image, the left eye is at the top-right of the black shape (row 9, col 44), and the right eye is in the middle-right (row 39, col 44).
Both eyes are now in the same column (col 44). This is interesting.
What if the goal is to have both eyes in the same column? Currently, both eyes are at col 44. Let me try pressing SPACE to see if this completes the level.
Actually, I already pressed SPACE after each RIGHT, and it didn't complete the level. So maybe the goal is not just to have both eyes in the same column.
What if the goal is to have both eyes at the SAME row AND same column? But the eyes can't overlap, so they can't be at the same position.
What if the goal is to have both eyes at the same row? Currently, the left eye is at row 9 and the right eye is at row 39. Let me try moving the left eye down to row 39, so both eyes are at row 39, col 44.
But wait, if both eyes are at row 39, col 44, they would overlap. So this is not allowed.
What if the goal is to have both eyes at the same row, but at different columns? Like, both eyes at row 39, but at cols 34 and 44?
Let me try moving the left eye down from row 9 to row 39. The left eye is at col 44, and it can move down from row 9 to row 14, 19, 24, 29, 34, 39, etc.
But if the left eye moves down to row 39, it would be at the same position as the right eye (row 39, col 44). So this is not allowed.
What if the left eye can only move down to row 34 (not 39), because the right eye is at row 39?
Let me try pressing DOWN and see what happens.
Actually, let me first try pressing LEFT to move the left eye left from col 44. If the left eye moves left, it would be at col 39, and the right eye would move right to col 49 (or stay at col 44 if it can't move right).
Let me try pressing LEFT and see what happens.

- Both eyes on right side (orange region)

- 245 actions total
Goal model: Maybe goal is both eyes at same row? Or need to move left eye down to match right eye's row?
Plan: Try DOWN to move left eye down toward row 39. Try LEFT to move eyes apart horizontally. Try SPACE at new positions.

seg = current_frame.segmentation
